title: buddleja asiatica var. densiflora
intro: Buddleja asiatica var. densiflora, also known as the Asian orka, is a deciduous shrub native to the Himalayan region and parts of China, characterized by its dense, compact growth habit and vibrant purple flowers. The plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine, with its leaves, flowers, and stems exhibiting anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Buddleja asiatica var. densiflora include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and alleviate respiratory problems, making it a valued remedy in traditional Chinese medicine. Herbal preparations of the plant often involve infusions of its leaves and flowers, which can be consumed as teas, or its leaves can be used in tinctures and infusions to support overall well-being.
